rows.notes: Clear page title: the year '1895' is centered and 'General Account' is written at top right in large legible script. No amounts on this header line.

This appears to be a section label at the left introducing the subsequent lines. The first word could be 'Due' or 'Dues' — the trailing loop looks like an 's', so recorded as 'Dues'. No monetary figures aligned on this text line.

Clear description 'Total Receipts' and boxed amount '67 11' immediately to the right. Pounds and shillings columns clearly aligned; no pence column entry visible (treated as 0). Values pass validation (shillings 0–19).

Description reads as an abbreviation 'Genl A/c' (interpreted as 'General Account'); script for abbreviation is slightly stylized but legible. Numeric columns align under the pounds/shillings/pence rulings showing '897 6 8'. All currency parts within valid ranges.
Description reads 'Balance, at beginning of acct.' (small abbreviation 'acct.'). Amount '4564 1 4' is well aligned in the three currency columns; digits are clear and within valid ranges.

Large final total written with currency symbol and boxed/underlined. Pounds '5528' and shillings '19' are clearly written; no pence shown (treated as 0). Alignment with subtotal lines confirms this is the total.
